The 'finalConfig' object makes the dependencies of useCallback Hook (at line 143) change on every render. To fix this, wrap the initialization of 'finalConfig' in its own useMemo() Hook.

React Hook useCallback has a missing dependency: 'themes'. Either include it or remove the dependency array.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [theme, themes]

messages.message: React Hook useCallback has missing dependencies: 'asciiLines' and 'themes'. Either include them or remove the dependency array.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [asciiLines, theme, themes]

messages.message: React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'initializeCharacterStates'. Either include it or remove the dependency array.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [initializeCharacterStates]

suppressedMessages.message: Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

suggestions.desc: Use `unknown` instead, this will force you to explicitly, and safely assert the type is correct.
Use `never` instead, this is useful when instantiating generic type parameters that you don't need to know the type of.

messages.message: React Hook useEffect was passed a dependency list that is not an array literal. This means we can't statically verify whether you've passed the correct dependencies.

The ref value 'imageRef.current' will likely have changed by the time this effect cleanup function runs. If this ref points to a node rendered by React, copy 'imageRef.current' to a variable inside the effect, and use that variable in the cleanup function.
